title: malacothamnus palmeri var. involucratus
intro: Malacothamnus palmeri var. involucratus, also known as the Palmer's malacothamnus or Palmer's bush mallow, is a variety of shrub in the mallow family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Malacothamnus palmeri var. involucratus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
